ipaddress/IPv4Network/hostmask.name: hostmask
ipaddress/IPv4Network/hostmask.description: The host mask, as an IPv4Address object.

info.excerpt: The host mask, as an IPv4Address object.

ipaddress/IPv4Network/hosts.name: hosts
ipaddress/IPv4Network/hosts.description: Returns an iterator over the usable hosts in the network. The usable hosts are all the IP addresses that belong to the network, except the network address itself and the network broadcast address. For networks with a mask length of 31, the network address and network broadcast address are also included in the result. Networks with a mask of 32 will return a list containing the single host address. >>> list(ip_network('192.0.2.0/29').hosts()) [IPv4Address('192.0.2.1'), IPv4Address('192.0.2.2')...

ipaddress/IPv6Network/hosts.name: hosts
ipaddress/IPv6Network/hosts.description: Returns an iterator over the usable hosts in the network. The usable hosts are all the IP addresses that belong to the network, except the Subnet-Router anycast address. For networks with a mask length of 127, the Subnet-Router anycast address is also included in the result. Networks with a mask of 128 will return a list containing the single host address.
